title: Capability security — sandboxed execution environments
purpose: A sandbox is a pre-configured capability set for common use cases: 'pure' (no I/O, no spawn, no network), 'worker' (network + file, no spawn), 'supervisor' (spawn + link, no direct I/O). Sandboxes make capability security accessible without manual bitfield construction.
task: Define standard sandbox profiles and a sandbox API.

requirements.title: Standard sandbox profiles
requirements.spec: Define named sandbox profiles as CapabilitySet presets.
acceptance: Sandbox::Worker — file I/O + network, no spawn
Sandbox::Supervisor — spawn + link + monitor, no direct I/O
Test: each sandbox profile has the documented capability set

requirements.title: Sandbox spawn API
requirements.spec: Ergonomic API for spawning processes in sandboxes.
acceptance: spawn_in_sandbox(sandbox, module, function, args) spawns with the sandbox's capabilities
Sandbox can be specified in spawn_opt options list
Test: spawn in Pure sandbox, verify NIF calls are denied

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement dynamic sandbox modification — fixed at spawn
SHALL NOT implement sandbox inheritance policies — children get parent's sandbox unless overridden
verification: cargo test -p beamr passes with all tests green
